libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: If tests exercise legacy Log4j 1 configuration-file compatibility, set the system property log4j1.compatibility=true and provide an explicit log4j.properties or log4j.xml test resource; isolate/reset Log4j state between tests because LogManager and logger configuration are global.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact POM marks log4j-core as optional, but normal bridge usage and many org.apache.log4j APIs route to org.apache.logging.log4j.core classes when present; without it, important compatibility operations such as programmatic levels, appenders, additivity, and Log4j 1 configuration support are 
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6772 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-1.2-api:2.18.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported PropertySetter assertion may still require a test or metadata change; adding log4j-core is setup for meaningful bridge coverage, not a guaranteed fix for that specific failure.
Specialized optional integrations such as JMS are not included; only add their APIs if tests deliberately cover those optional classes.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: log4j-1.2-api is a Log4j 1.x compatibility bridge whose meaningful logger, appender, hierarchy, level, and configuration paths depend on the optional Log4j Core backend; the resolved artifact only brings log4j-api transitively.
